verdict_reasoning: HSPA5 (GRP78/BiP) is canonically an ER-lumenal chaperone with a KDEL retention signal, resident in the ER under normal conditions. However, well-documented stress-induced translocation to the cell surface occurs during ER stress, viral infection, and in cancer cells (including prostate, ovarian, and lung cancers). Multiple published reviews explicitly describe 'cell surface HSPA5' as a therapeutic target, with antibody-based strategies, and it has been described as a viral entry receptor. Surface translocation is condition-dependent (ER stress / tumorigenesis), making this a clear contextual verdict via cell_state_induced mechanism.
